cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. I need to figure out which statement is true based on the passage. The passage talks about memory tricks. The first example is about the writer finding the front door unlocked in the morning, even though they remember locking it the night before. So the key part here is: "One morning last week, for example, I got up and found that I had left the front door unlocked all night, yet I clearly remember locking it carefully the night before." Looking at the options: A) One night the writer forgot to lock the front door. But the passage says they found the door unlocked, but they remember locking it. So maybe they actually didn't lock it, but thought they did. So the writer forgot to lock it, but the passage is about remembering something that didn't happen. Wait,
